title: AI Content Workspace · Settings (MVP)
summary: A revised, product-native /content workspace for Searchify built inside the real app shell (240px grouped sidebar with Content active under Actions, 52px top bar, 1440px content region) using the exact globals.css tokens verbatim: teal-green accent #0f9d76 (light) / #2dd4a7 (dark), the neutral surface hierarchy, 4px spacing rhythm, thin borders, restrained shadows, and mono tabular numerals for word counts and timestamps. References 7.png/4.png informed hierarchy only — a centered 'What can I help you create?' prompt hero with an integrated action bar — not their blue-violet palette or their multi-connector/social-type framing. Scope is deliberately narrowed to the clarified first version: a prominent AI prompt textarea with a single Website-page output label, a single optional tool integration (Website context, showing the active project BooksToScrape with a clear on-switch), and a Generate action. Deliberately NOT present: no provider/AI-model picker (provider is env-driven, Mistral by default, never a user control), no GitHub/Notion/CMS connectors, no attachments, no multiple social/content types, no publish or Draft/Published workflow, and no filters. A persistent hint states the site is reference context to ground the draft, not instructions to follow verbatim, and the result card repeats this as provenance. The plan covers the three states a generation feature has. 'Prompt & ready' shows the zero-data empty/ready state — the large hero prompt, Website-page label, active Website-context toggle, Generate, and an empty generation-history card; its recommended variant is dark, matching the product's default theme observed on the running app. 'Generated result' shows a persisted draft with a status/type/context badge row, relative time and word count (mono), Copy and Regenerate actions, a full persisted provenance grid (provider Mistral, returned/requested model, input/output token usage, latency, source crawl timestamp) plus a visible truncation warning and an AI-generated disclaimer reinforcing context-not-instructions, and a prior-generation list rail of truncated prompts with the active item highlighted. 'Transient states' lays out the bounded generating affordance (locked composer, indeterminate progress bar + skeleton with no fabricated ETA or percentage, Cancel) beside the recoverable error affordance (dismissable alert, editable prompt, Try again, prompt and context preserved; Dismiss returns to the editable ready composer keeping the prompt and Website context set). The Content nav item is live across every mockup — including the Settings shell — because the feature ships globally, so it carries no SOON pill anywhere. Every variation ships as separate light and dark HTML files (no in-file theme toggle); responsive narrow behavior collapses the sidebar and stacks the result rail, focus rings and ARIA roles (switch, progressbar, status, alert) are wired throughout. The two Settings sections are preserved unchanged; the obsolete local-scratchpad Content sections have been removed.

sections.title: AI Content — Prompt & ready
sections.description: The empty/ready AI Content workspace: the 'What can I help you create?' prompt hero with a Website-page label, active Website-context toggle, Generate, and an empty generation history.

sections.title: AI Content — Generated result
sections.description: A persisted generated website page rendered as sanitized Markdown, with a full provenance grid (Mistral, returned/requested model, token usage, latency, source crawl time), a truncation warning, an AI-generated disclaimer, Copy and Regenerate, and a prior-generation list of truncated prompts.

sections.title: AI Content — Transient states
sections.description: The bounded generating affordance (locked composer, indeterminate progress, Cancel) and the recoverable error affordance (editable prompt, Try again, prompt and context preserved; Dismiss clears the alert and returns to the editable ready composer with prompt and Website context still set).

sections.title: Settings — Account
sections.description: A basic /settings screen with a minimal sub-nav, read-only account essentials (account role, created time, user ID), an appearance preference, and a Configuration card linking out to Provider Settings and Project Setup.

sections.title: Sidebar user dropdown — Settings above Sign out
sections.description: The sidebar account menu opening upward with a Settings item placed directly above Sign out.
